root_text.an5.18:1.1: “Pañcahi, bhikkhave, dhammehi samannāgato bhikkhu parahitāya paṭipanno hoti, no attahitāya.
root_text.an5.18:1.3: Idha, bhikkhave, bhikkhu attanā na sīlasampanno hoti, paraṁ sīlasampadāya samādapeti;
root_text.an5.18:1.4: attanā na samādhisampanno hoti, paraṁ samādhisampadāya samādapeti;
root_text.an5.18:1.5: attanā na paññāsampanno hoti, paraṁ paññāsampadāya samādapeti;
root_text.an5.18:1.6: attanā na vimuttisampanno hoti, paraṁ vimuttisampadāya samādapeti;
root_text.an5.18:1.7: attanā na vimuttiñāṇadassanasampanno hoti, paraṁ vimuttiñāṇadassanasampadāya samādapeti.
root_text.an5.18:1.8: Imehi kho, bhikkhave, pañcahi dhammehi samannāgato bhikkhu parahitāya paṭipanno hoti, no attahitāyā”ti.

translation_text.an5.18:1.1: “Mendicants, a mendicant with five qualities is practicing for the welfare of others, but not their own.
translation_text.an5.18:1.3: It’s when a mendicant is not personally accomplished in ethics, immersion, wisdom, freedom, or the knowledge and vision of freedom. But they encourage others in these qualities.
translation_text.an5.18:1.8: A mendicant with these five qualities is practicing for the welfare of others, but not their own.”
